commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in ACOS-controlled execution mode using new environment flags and provenance metadata across agent, Gateway, channel, cron, shell, and apply_patch paths, plus docs and protocol/Swift model fields.

PR surface: Source +255, Tests +173, Docs +58, Other +8. Total +494 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible for the PR findings: the PR schema accepts `acosProvenance`, the Gateway forwards it to ingress, and the validator lets diagnostic-mode mutating actions continue when provenance exists. I did not run a live controlled-mode Gateway or Telegram setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because this new security/control-plane mode has trust-model blockers and missing contributor real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the author.

target.title: feat: add ACOS-controlled mode for OpenClaw containment
target.body:
## Purpose

Adds ACOS-controlled mode so external OpenClaw can be run as an ACOS-governed execution surface instead of an unmanaged autonomous assistant.

Required note: OPENCLAW_ACOS_CONTROLLED=1 is opt-in. Existing OpenClaw behavior remains unchanged unless controlled mode is enabled.

## Entry points guarded

- Local agent execution through the agent command boundary.
- Gateway agent execution and Gateway protocol agent params.
- Channel dispatch turns, including Telegram-style inbound dispatch.
- Cron-created agent turns and isolated cron agent execution.

## Dangerous tools guarded

- Shell execution through exec tool defaults.
- apply_patch execution.
- Dangerous action classes now require ACOS approval metadata when controlled mode is enabled.

## Controlled-mode behavior

When OPENCLAW_ACOS_CONTROLLED=1 or OPENCLAW_DISABLE_AUTONOMOUS_INTAKE=1 is set, unmanaged mutating agent execution is rejected unless valid ACOS provenance is present. Diagnostic mode remains limited to non-mutating checks.

## Provenance validator purity change

src/acos/provenance.ts is a pure leaf module. It does not import gateway, channel, cron, shell, repo, logging, config runtime, infra/env, or package alias modules. Local minimal env flag parsing is implemented inside the module.

## Validation passed

- node --import tsx provenance import check: passed.
- Minimal in-repo Vitest config for src/acos/provenance.test.ts: 11 passed.
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 on changed TS files: passed.
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json on changed TS files: passed.
- Targeted containment import checks: passed.

## Normal Vitest status

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acos/provenance.test.ts: passed, 1 file / 11 tests.

## Remaining bypass risks

- Controlled mode is opt-in and requires OpenClaw processes to be started with the controlled-mode environment flag.
- ACOS provenance is metadata-based; it is not cryptographically signed in this patch.
- Existing processes already running without controlled mode are not retroactively contained.
- External sends outside the guarded agent/channel/tool paths were not exhaustively redesigned in this patch.

## Safe operating rule

Run OpenClaw as an ACOS subordinate only with OPENCLAW_ACOS_CONTROLLED=1 enabled and ACOS-issued provenance supplied for mutating or agent-execution paths.

## Merge condition

Ready to merge after reviewer acceptance and CI confirmation.
target.author: ashreim-upl
